title: Understanding Negative Numbers on a Number Line

steps.instruction: On a number line, find 0. Numbers to the right are positive, numbers to the left are negative.
steps.explanation: Zero is the dividing point. Positive numbers are right, negative numbers are left.

steps.work: -4 is 4 units to the left of 0.
steps.explanation: Negative numbers represent values less than zero. They are used for temperatures below zero, depths below sea level, debts, etc.

steps.work: The opposite of -4 is 4. Both are 4 units from 0.
steps.explanation: Every number has an opposite. The opposite has the same distance from 0 but on the other side.

steps.instruction: The weather report says the temperature is -8°C. What does this mean?
steps.explanation: The negative sign tells us the temperature is below the freezing/zero point.

steps.work: -8 is farther below 0 than -2, so -8°C is colder.
steps.explanation: With negative temperatures, the number with the larger absolute value is colder (farther below zero).

steps.instruction: If the temperature rises 12 degrees from -8°C, what is the new temperature?
steps.explanation: Starting at -8 and going up 12 brings us to 4°C (above zero).
